body:
## Rationale

The `/articles` listing hub has **full SSR meta + CollectionPage schema** already in `api/meta/[...path].js` (confirmed: `if (path === '/articles')` handler at line ~1050) but is **completely absent from sitemap.js** — same bug class as `/stats` (#1789), `/beginner-guide` (#1801), and `/cards` (#1804), all recently promoted.

```bash
grep "'/articles'" api/sitemap.js  # returns 0 — confirmed MISSING
```

The individual article pages ARE sitemapped (`/articles/\<slug>`) but the hub index is invisible to crawlers. The hub at `/articles` surfaces 100+ album articles, serves as the canonical CollectionPage, and would benefit from direct Google crawl discovery.

## Impact

- Immediate: Google discovers the hub page and can pass PageRank to the 102 individual article URLs
- `/articles` hub = CollectionPage linking to $10k+ of editorial content → improves domain authority flow
- Same one-line fix as #1789 (#1789 promoted 5★ by CEO on 2026-06-19)

## Files to touch

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/sitemap.js` | Add `{ loc: '/articles', priority: '0.9', changefreq: 'weekly' }` to static pages array |

No SSR meta or schema changes needed — both already exist.

## Verify

1. `node api/sitemap.js | grep '/articles'` → returns `<loc>https://metalforge.io/articles</loc>`
2. Google Search Console → URL Inspection → `/articles` → "URL is on Google" within 7 days
3. No duplicate or conflicting entries with `/articles/<slug>`
4. CollectionPage schema still renders in `curl -s https://metalforge.io/api/meta?path=/articles | grep CollectionPage`
5. schema.org rich results validator confirms valid CollectionPage at `/articles`

## Done

`/articles` hub appears in sitemap.xml and is indexable by Google and AI crawlers.
title: SEO: /articles hub page — add to sitemap.js (CollectionPage schema exists, zero crawl discoverability)
